title: message_query and message_get guard preconditions before any network call
description: message_query returns ClientError::InvalidArgument immediately if neither chat_id nor has_mention=true is provided. message_get returns InvalidArgument immediately if ids is empty. A contributor might remove these guards on the grounds that the server will reject the request anyway. The guards are correct because: (1) the server error path requires a round-trip and may return a vague unsupportedFilter rather than a clear message; (2) the client tests for these guards use no mock server, proving the guard fires before any I/O — a valuable property for fast unit testing.
